mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits immediately to equity valuations through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because real-rate relief then dominates inflation fear.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month, or if the sign flips during periods where CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals labor-market deterioration and weaker future household income, which depresses equity expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: A broad consumer-demand pickup shown by higher RSAFS combined with rising PAYEMS indicates stronger current nominal activity and supports higher near-term corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone, or if 5-day SPX returns are not positive in the majority of interaction-triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO while HOUST remains weak indicates supply-side improvement without overheating in housing demand, which supports cyclical risk appetite and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by at least 0.5% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances fail to show positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when HOUST is also rising sharply.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays near the lower bound, real policy becomes more accommodative and liquidity conditions improve, which should push S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.4% over 2 months while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation, low-rate trigger episodes do not produce above-average 20-day SPX gains, or if the sign is negative in more than half of such cases.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 after a prior expansion signals demand deceleration and earnings downgrade risk, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is slow-moving and becomes visible only after the GDP impulse is confirmed.
failure_condition: Reject if quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 contractions are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal loses predictive power in three consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in UNRATE and stagnation in PAYEMS identifies an employment slowdown that weakens consumption expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment rises but payrolls continue to expand.
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration episodes do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns, or if payroll gains offset unemployment rises and the sign remains negative anyway.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary industrial rebound, rising INDPRO with flat CPIAUCSL supports margins by lowering unit-cost pressure while output improves, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism requires both real activity improvement and contained inflation.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises over 2 consecutive months and CPIAUCSL increases less than 0.2% over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the trigger does not correspond to positive 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL accelerates.
